    35|

    39|  PRESENT_BUT_INVALID: Colors.error,
    40|  PRESENT_BUT_UNAUTHORIZED: Colors.error,

    42|  MISSING_FROM_RORK: Colors.error,
    43|};
    44|
    45|const STATUS_LABEL: Record<VarStatus, string> = {
    46|  VERIFIED: 'VERIFIED',
    47|  PRESENT_IN_RUNTIME: 'PRESENT IN RUNTIME',
    48|  PRESENT_BUT_INVALID: 'PRESENT BUT INVALID',
    49|  PRESENT_BUT_UNAUTHORIZED: 'UNAUTHORIZED',
    50|  PRESENT_IN_RORK_NOT_INJECTED: 'IN RORK · NOT INJECTED',
    51|  MISSING_FROM_RORK: 'MISSING FROM RORK',
    52|};
    53|
    54|function StatusIcon({ status }: { status: VarStatus }) {
    55|  if (status === 'VERIFIED') return <CheckCircle2 size={16} color={Colors.success} />;
    56|  if (status === 'PRESENT_IN_RUNTIME' || status === 'PRESENT_IN_RORK_NOT_INJECTED') {
    57|    return <AlertTriangle size={16} color={Colors.warning} />;
    58|  }
    59|  return <XCircle size={16} color={Colors.error} />;
    60|}
    61|
    62|function VariableCard({
    63|  variable,
    64|  onSync,
    65|  syncing,
    66|}: {
    67|  variable: RuntimeVariableStatus;
    68|  onSync: (name: string) => void;
    69|  syncing: boolean;
    70|}) {
    71|  const color = STATUS_COLOR[variable.status];
    72|  const onCopyMasked = useCallback(() => {
    73|    if (variable.masked) void Clipboard.setStringAsync(variable.masked);
    74|  }, [variable.masked]);
    75|
    76|  return (
    77|    <View style={[styles.varCard, { borderColor: color }]}>
    78|      <View style={styles.varHeaderRow}>
    79|        <StatusIcon status={variable.status} />
    80|        <Text style={styles.varName} numberOfLines={1}>{variable.name}</Text>
    81|        <View style={[styles.statusPill, { backgroundColor: color }]}>
    82|          <Text style={styles.statusPillText}>{STATUS_LABEL[variable.status]}</Text>
    83|        </View>
    84|      </View>
    85|
    86|      <Text style={styles.varDesc} numberOfLines={3}>{variable.description}</Text>
    87|
    88|      {variable.publicWarning ? (

    91|          <Text style={styles.publicWarnText}>
    92|            Public variable — inlined into the client bundle. Never store a secret here.
    93|          </Text>
    94|        </View>
    95|      ) : null}
    96|
    97|      <View style={styles.metaGrid}>
    98|        <View style={styles.metaItem}>
    99|          <Text style={styles.metaLabel}>Scope</Text>

   101|        </View>
   102|        <View style={styles.metaItem}>
   103|          <Text style={styles.metaLabel}>Value</Text>
   104|          <Text style={styles.metaValue}>{variable.present ? `${variable.masked} (${variable.valueLength})` : '—'}</Text>

responseMessageContents.text:
The user wants a "Security Box" for GitHub and Render — a live panel that shows the security status of both platforms. I have a clear picture now. Let me build a new IVX screen `expo/app/ivx/security-box.tsx` that fetches from the existing backend endpoints:

- `/api/ivx/deploy-tools/github` → token, permissions, branches, latest commit
- `/api/ivx/deploy-tools/render` → service status, deploys, env vars count
- `/api/ivx/deploy-tools/credentials` → masked credentials with validation status

responseMessageContents.text:
Build passed. Here's what's now live in the IVX app.

## Security Box · GitHub + Render

New screen: **`/ivx/security-box`** — a live, verified security posture panel for both production deployment platforms. No fabricated status, no exposed secrets.

### What it shows

**Top summary card** — at-a-glance state for both platforms:
- GitHub: `READY` / `BLOCKED` / `DEGRADED`
- Render: `READY` / `BLOCKED` / `DEGRADED`
- Credentials valid vs total, missing count

**GitHub panel**
- Live token state banner (exact error if BLOCKED)
- Token permissions probed against the real API: repo access, can push, read workflows, read secrets, workflow access, admin
- Repository: default branch, branch count, protected branches, latest commit SHA + message + timestamp

**Render panel**
- Live service state banner
- Service: name, type, branch, auto-deploy on/off, suspended status, env var count
- Latest deploy: deploy id, status (`LIVE` / `BUILD_FAILED` / etc.), commit SHA, failure reason, created/finished timestamps

**Credentials section** (per platform + Supabase/AWS/AI/security)
- Each credential: name, REQUIRED pill, validation state (`VALID` / `MISSING` / `AUTH_FAILED` / `EXPIRED` / `UNVERIFIED`), validation detail, sources present, whether it was live-probed
- Credential gaps list with exact missing-required names

**Blockers** — honest, copyable list of exact blockers per platform (e.g. `GITHUB_TOKEN — REQUIRED credential missing`, `Render service suspended: ...`). Copy button per platform.

### Truth-first guarantees
- No secret values are ever fetched or displayed — backend returns only masked presence + validation status
- State is `BLOCKED` with the exact blocker when credentials are missing/expired/invalid — never `READY` without verification
- Pull-to-refresh re-probes all three endpoints in parallel

### Entry points
- IVX Inbox → "Security Box · GitHub + Render" link (with ShieldCheck icon)
- Direct route: `/ivx/security-box`

### Backend (already existed, now consumed)
- `GET /api/ivx/deploy-tools/github` — token, permissions, branches, commit
- `GET /api/ivx/deploy-tools/render` — service, deploys, env vars
- `GET /api/ivx/deploy-tools/credentials` — masked credential sync across all sources

**Validation:** `runChecks` passed — TypeScript strict, no errors.

Want me to add an owner-approval-gated "Rotate token" action or wire this into the Owner Chat's `STATE: BLOCKED` responses so blockers link straight to this screen?

responseMessageContents.title: Added a new security status page to check GitHub and Render connections.
responseMessageContents.description: Created a Security Box screen where you can see the live connection status for GitHub and Render. This page shows if your login tokens are working and identifies any missing settings or blockers. You can now access this screen directly from your inbox to verify that everything is configured correctly for deployments.
